subject: grammar
title: Relative Clauses: Match and Join
instruction: Join the sentence beginnings (1-10) with the endings (a-j) by adding a relative pronoun (who, which, that, where, when, whose, why).
passage:
Example: The college has many students who are classed as 'mature' because they are over 21.

1 My cousin Phillip is a solicitor; he was the one 1 [1].
2 I went to a school 2 [2].
3 I visited the theatre 3 [3].
4 Why don't you call again at a time 4 [4].
5 Mrs Jackson is the kind of teacher 5 [5].
6 Faraday was the man 6 [6].
7 Is there any reason 7 [7].
8 What was the name of that company 8 [8].
9 That's the woman 9 [9].

Options to match:
a. Mozart performed many of his operas.
b. flat I rent.
c. every student wants to have.
e. advised me to study law.
f. I'm not as busy.
g. invented the first electric motor.
h. factory burned down yesterday?
i. didn't have very good sports facilities.
j. manufacturers like to keep demand above supply?

questions.answer: who advised me to study law|||that advised me to study law
which didn't have very good sports facilities|||that didn't have very good sports facilities
where Mozart performed many of his operas
when I'm not as busy|||that I'm not as busy

questions.text: 5. Mrs Jackson is the kind of teacher:
questions.answer: who every student wants to have|||that every student wants to have|||every student wants to have
who invented the first electric motor|||that invented the first electric motor
why manufacturers like to keep demand above supply|||manufacturers like to keep demand above supply
